blurb: A hybrid Echinopsis whose growing tip has fanned sideways into a thick, wavy green crest instead of a column — a cristate mutation. Nursery-tagged as the cross 'TPM × Althea'; a proper sculptural oddity.

care.light: Bright light with plenty of direct sun keeps the crest firm and well-coloured; too little and the fan grows soft and pale. Acclimate before a big jump in sun.
care.water: Soak-and-dry — a good drink in warm growth, then dry before the next. Keep it dry through winter, and never let water sit in the folds of the crest.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~35°F and dry when cool.
care.dormancy: A summer grower; rest it cool and dry in winter.
care.feeding: A dilute low-nitrogen cactus feed once or twice in summer.
care.potting: Repot infrequently; keep the mix low around the base so water drains away from the crest. Many crests are grafted — check the base.
care.propagation: By cutting and rooting a section of the crest (fiddly), or grafting; it may also throw normal (reverted) columnar growth to propagate from.
care.bloom: Hybrid Echinopsis are bred for big, showy, often fragrant flowers — a crest may still push the odd large bloom.
care.troubles: Rot from water trapped in the crest's folds; sunburn if rushed; mealybugs and spider mites.
care.toxicity: Non-toxic — but handle the spiny crest with care.
